Name: curvature-aware_precision_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing: Training-Free Representation Geometry for Efficient Inference
Short Hypothesis: Precision routing strategies based on curvature-aware representation geometry can significantly improve inference efficiency without requiring additional training, by dynamically allocating computational resources based on the local curvature of the loss landscape.
Related Work: Existing precision routing methods often rely on heuristic approaches or require extensive training to adapt to different computational budgets. Recent work has explored curvature-aware techniques in optimization and inference, but these have not been systematically applied to precision routing. Our proposal distinguishes itself by leveraging training-free curvature diagnostics to inform precision allocation, ensuring both computational efficiency and theoretical rigor.
Abstract: We propose a novel framework for curvature-aware precision routing that dynamically allocates computational resources based on the local curvature of the loss landscape. By utilizing training-free curvature diagnostics, our approach eliminates the need for additional training and ensures efficient inference. We introduce a deterministic routing protocol that leverages spectral estimators and Hessian conditioning to quantify feature entanglement and gradient covariance decay. This protocol replaces heuristic precision allocation with formalized stability radii and explicit condition number constraints, optimizing the throughput-accuracy Pareto front for single-GPU deployments. Our experiments demonstrate significant improvements in inference efficiency across various architectures, validated through rigorous empirical evaluation and multi-seed variance quantification.
Experiments: Experiment 1: Evaluate the impact of curvature-aware precision routing on inference efficiency for ResNet-50 and Transformer models. Metrics: Inference time, accuracy, and computational overhead.
Experiment 2: Compare the proposed method against state-of-the-art precision routing techniques. Metrics: Throughput, accuracy, and resource utilization.
Experiment 3: Validate the robustness of curvature diagnostics across different datasets and architectures. Metrics: Variance in diagnostic accuracy and computational overhead.
Experiment 4: Assess the scalability of the proposed framework to larger models and datasets. Metrics: Inference time and resource utilization.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The effectiveness of curvature diagnostics may vary across different architectures and datasets.
Deterministic routing protocols may introduce additional computational overhead if not properly optimized.
The framework relies on accurate Hessian conditioning, which may be challenging to compute for very large models.
Potential limitations in generalizability to non-convex optimization landscapes.
